title: Raj Empire Esports BGMI Tournament Website - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Build player registration page with mobile, display name, BGMI Player ID, OTP verification, T&C checkbox, and duplicate mobile enforcement
acceptanceCriteria: Form has mobile number, display name, and BGMI Player ID fields
After submitting mobile, OTP step is shown with the simulated code displayed on screen
Duplicate mobile number shows a clear error message
T&C checkbox is present with a link to /terms
Submit is disabled until checkbox is checked
On successful registration, player is redirected to /dashboard

file_operations.description: Create the player registration page with multi-step form (mobile → OTP → details). Collect mobile number, display name, and BGMI Player ID. Call backend generateOtp() and display the simulated OTP on screen. Call verifyOtp() to validate the OTP. Call registerPlayer() to save the account. Enforce duplicate mobile error handling. Include a checkbox for agreeing to Terms & Conditions with a clickable link to /terms. Disable submit button until checkbox is checked. On success, redirect to /dashboard. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents and angular UI elements.
Add route for /register that renders RegisterPage component. Ensure the route is accessible without authentication.

requirements.summary: Build player login page with mobile + OTP authentication
acceptanceCriteria: Login page has mobile number input and OTP step
Simulated OTP is displayed on screen after mobile submission
Correct OTP entry logs the player in and redirects to /dashboard
Incorrect OTP shows an error message

file_operations.description: Create the player login page with mobile number input followed by OTP verification step. Call backend generateOtp() and display the simulated OTP on screen. Call verifyOtp() to authenticate. On success, store player session (mobile number or player ID) in localStorage or context and redirect to /dashboard. Show error message for incorrect OTP. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents.
Add route for /login that renders LoginPage component. Ensure the route is accessible without authentication.

Create an authentication context to manage player login state. Store logged-in player information (mobile number, display name, BGMI Player ID) and provide login/logout functions. Use localStorage to persist session across page reloads.
Wrap the App component with AuthContext provider to make authentication state available throughout the app.

requirements.summary: Build tournament listing page showing all active tournaments with registration capability
acceptanceCriteria: All active tournaments render as cards with all specified fields
Match Rules section renders below card only when matchRules is non-empty
Register button is disabled and shows 'Full' when filledSlots equals totalSlots
Unauthenticated players are prompted to log in when clicking Register

file_operations.description: Create the tournament listing page that fetches all tournaments from the backend (use getTournamentsByMap or appropriate query method from backend interface). Display each tournament as a card showing: name, entry fee, prize pool, total slots, filled slots, map, date/time, and status (Open/Full). If matchRules is non-empty, display it below the card. Disable the Register button and show 'Full' when filledSlots equals totalSlots. For unauthenticated players, show a prompt to log in when clicking Register. On Register click (for logged-in players), navigate to the payment modal or page. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents and angular card designs.
Add route for /tournaments that renders TournamentsPage component. Make this route accessible without authentication for browsing, but require login for registration actions.

requirements.summary: Implement payment flow with UPI ID, QR code display, payment screenshot upload, and T&C checkbox
acceptanceCriteria: Payment modal/page shows UPI ID and QR code image
Falls back to upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png when no QR is uploaded
Player can upload a payment screenshot image file
T&C checkbox must be checked before submission
Registration is saved with status Pending Admin Approval
Room ID and Password are not visible until admin approves

file_operations.description: Create a payment modal component that accepts a tournament object as prop. Display the tournament's UPI ID (default to 'empirerajesports@ibl' if not set) and QR code. Use the blob-storage component to handle QR code display: if tournament.qrCodeBlob exists, use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() to show the image; otherwise, fall back to frontend/public/assets/generated/upi-qr-placeholder.dim_300x300.png. Include a file input for the player to upload a payment screenshot. Use ExternalBlob.fromBytes() from the blob-storage component to convert the selected file to ExternalBlob before submission. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Include a checkbox for agreeing to Terms & Conditions with a link to /terms. Disable submit until the checkbox is checked. On submission, call backend registerForTournament(tournamentId, paymentScreenshotBlob) with the ExternalBlob instance. Show success message indicating 'Pending Admin Approval'. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents.
Integrate PaymentModal component. When a logged-in player clicks Register on a tournament card, open the PaymentModal and pass the selected tournament. Handle modal open/close state.

requirements.summary: Build player dashboard showing profile, tournament registrations with Room ID/Password for approved registrations, and support ticket management
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows player display name and BGMI Player ID
Tournament registrations are listed with correct status badges
Room ID and Room Password are visible only for Approved registrations
Support ticket creation form with subject, description, and optional screenshot upload works
Ticket list shows subject, status badge, and creation date
Clicking a ticket opens full detail view with admin reply if present

file_operations.description: Create the player dashboard page showing player profile (display name and BGMI Player ID from AuthContext). Display the player's tournament registrations with status badges (Pending/Approved/Rejected). For Approved registrations, show Room ID and Room Password. Include a Support section with a form to create new support tickets (subject, description, optional screenshot upload). Use the blob-storage component (ExternalBlob.fromBytes()) for screenshot upload. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Call backend createSupportTicket() with the screenshot as ExternalBlob or null. Display the player's ticket list with subject, status badge, and creation date. Make each ticket clickable to view full details including admin reply if present. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents.
Add route for /dashboard that renders DashboardPage component. Protect this route with authentication guard: redirect to /login if not authenticated.
Create a ProtectedRoute wrapper component that checks AuthContext for logged-in player. If not authenticated, redirect to /login. Otherwise, render the child component.

requirements.summary: Build admin dashboard with hardcoded login, tournament management, registrations approval, player list, T&C editor, support ticket management, and social links editor
acceptanceCriteria: Admin login at /admin accepts only username 'Empire Esports' and password 'Shivam803119&'
Tournament Management section supports add/edit/delete with all fields including matchRules
Registrations section lists all registrations with payment screenshots and approve/reject actions
Player List section shows all registered players
T&C section shows current content in textarea with a functional save button
Support Tickets section lists all tickets and allows reply and status update
Social Links section has YouTube, Instagram, Telegram URL inputs with save

file_operations.description: Create the admin login page at route /admin with hardcoded credentials check: username 'Empire Esports' and password 'Shivam803119&'. On successful login, store admin session in localStorage or context and redirect to /admin/dashboard. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents.

Create the admin dashboard page with six sections: (1) Tournament Management — add/edit/delete tournaments with all fields (name, dateTime, entryFee, prizePool, map, totalSlots, upiId, matchRules textarea, QR code upload using blob-storage component, roomId, roomPassword). Use ExternalBlob.fromBytes() for QR code upload and ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for display. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Call backend createTournament() and appropriate update/delete methods. (2) Registrations — list all registrations with player info and payment screenshots (use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() to display). Provide approve/reject buttons. (3) Player List — display all registered players. (4) T&C — fetch current content via backend getTermsAndConditions(), display in textarea, and call updateTermsAndConditions() on save. (5) Support Tickets — fetch all tickets via getAllSupportTicketsSorted(), display with player name, BGMI ID, subject, status, date. Click to view detail with screenshot (use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() if present). Provide reply input and status update dropdown/buttons. (6) Social Links — fetch via getSocialLinks(), display input fields for YouTube, Instagram, Telegram URLs, and call updateSocialLinks() on save. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents.
Add route for /admin that renders AdminLoginPage component. Add route for /admin/dashboard that renders AdminDashboardPage component and is protected with an admin authentication guard.
Create an admin authentication context to manage admin login state. Store admin session (e.g., isAdminLoggedIn boolean) and provide login/logout functions. Use localStorage to persist session.
Wrap the App component with AdminAuthContext provider in addition to AuthContext.
Create an AdminProtectedRoute wrapper component that checks AdminAuthContext for admin login. If not authenticated, redirect to /admin. Otherwise, render the child component.

requirements.summary: Add dedicated Terms & Conditions page displaying content fetched from backend
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /terms fetches and renders T&C content from backend
Page uses the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents
Page is accessible via the link in registration and payment forms

file_operations.description: Create the Terms & Conditions page that fetches T&C content from the backend using getTermsAndConditions() and displays it. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents and readable typography. Ensure the page is accessible without authentication.
Add route for /terms that renders TermsPage component. Ensure the route is accessible without authentication.

requirements.summary: Apply dark battle-royale gaming aesthetic, Raj Empire Esports branding, logo, and social links in footer across all pages
acceptanceCriteria: All pages use near-black background with orange and gold accents
Sharp angular UI elements and military/tactical typography are applied throughout
Tab title, header, and footer all read 'Raj Empire Esports'
raj-empire-esports-logo.dim_400x120.png is used as the site header logo
Footer displays YouTube, Instagram, and Telegram icon links fetched from backend

file_operations.description: Update global styles to apply the dark battle-royale gaming aesthetic: near-black background (#0a0a0a or similar), orange (#ff6b00 or similar) and gold (#ffd700 or similar) accent colors. Use sharp angular UI elements (e.g., border styles, card shapes). Apply military/tactical typography (e.g., bold sans-serif fonts like 'Rajdhani', 'Orbitron', or 'Saira Condensed'). Ensure consistent theme across all components and pages.

Extend Tailwind theme with custom colors for the dark gaming aesthetic: near-black background, orange and gold accents. Add custom font families for military/tactical typography if needed. Configure utilities for sharp angular UI elements.
Update the page title to 'Raj Empire Esports' in the <title> tag. Add any necessary font imports (e.g., Google Fonts for military-style typography).

Create a Header component displaying the site logo using frontend/public/assets/generated/raj-empire-esports-logo.dim_400x120.png. Include navigation links to /tournaments, /login, /register, and /dashboard (if logged in). Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents and angular design. Display 'Raj Empire Esports' branding.

Create a Footer component displaying 'Raj Empire Esports' branding. Fetch social links from the backend using getSocialLinks() and display YouTube, Instagram, and Telegram icon links. Use appropriate icon library (e.g., react-icons) for social media icons. Use the dark gaming theme with orange/gold accents.
Create a Layout component that wraps all pages with Header and Footer components. Use this Layout in App.tsx to ensure consistent branding and navigation across all pages.
Wrap all page routes with the Layout component to ensure Header and Footer are displayed on every page.
